english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Infirmum autem in fide assumite, non in disceptationibus cogitationum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν δὲ ἀσθενοῦντα τῇ πίστει προσλαμβάνεσθε μὴ εἰς διακρίσεις διαλογισμῶν
verses.king_james: Him that is weak in the faith receive ye, *but* not to doubtful disputations.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alius enim credit se manducare omnia: qui autem infirmus est, olus manducet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς μὲν πιστεύει φαγεῖν πάντα ὁ δὲ ἀσθενῶν λάχανα ἐσθίει
verses.king_james: For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth herbs.

verses.latin_vulgate: Is qui manducat, non manducantem non spernat: et qui non manducat, manducantem non judicet: Deus enim illum assumpsit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἐσθίων τὸν μὴ ἐσθίοντα μὴ ἐξουθενείτω καὶ ὁ μὴ ἐσθίων τὸν ἐσθίοντα μὴ κρινέτω ὁ θεὸς γὰρ αὐτὸν προσελάβετο
verses.king_james: Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu quis es, qui judicas alienum servum? domino suo stat, aut cadit: stabit autem: potens est enim Deus statuere illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ τίς εἶ ὁ κρίνων ἀλλότριον οἰκέτην τῷ ἰδίῳ κυρίῳ στήκει ἢ πίπτει σταθήσεται δέ, δυνατὸς γάρ ἐστιν ὁ φεὸς στῆσαι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Who art thou that judgest another man's servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam alius judicat diem inter diem: alius autem judicat omnem diem: unusquisque in suo sensu abundet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς μὲν κρίνει ἡμέραν παρ᾽ ἡμέραν, ὃς δὲ κρίνει πᾶσαν ἡμέραν ἕκαστος ἐν τῷ ἰδίῳ νοῒ πληροφορείσθω
verses.king_james: One man esteemeth one day above another: another esteemeth every day *alike*. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui sapit diem, Domino sapit, et qui manducat, Domino manducat: gratias enim agit Deo. Et qui non manducat, Domino non manducat, et gratias agit Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ φρονῶν τὴν ἡμέραν κυρίῳ φρονεῖ καὶ ὁ μὴ φρονῶν τὴν ἡμέραν, κυρίῳ οὐ φρονεῖ ὁ ἐσθίων κυρίῳ ἐσθίει εὐχαριστεῖ γὰρ τῷ θεῷ καὶ ὁ μὴ ἐσθίων κυρίῳ οὐκ ἐσθίει καὶ εὐχαριστεῖ τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: He that regardeth the day, regardeth *it* unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard *it*. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo enim nostrum sibi vivit, et nemo sibi moritur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς γὰρ ἡμῶν ἑαυτῷ ζῇ καὶ οὐδεὶς ἑαυτῷ ἀποθνῄσκει
verses.king_james: For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sive enim vivemus, Domino vivimus: sive morimur, Domino morimur. Sive ergo vivimus, sive morimur, Domini sumus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐάν τε γὰρ ζῶμεν τῷ κυρίῳ ζῶμεν ἐάν τε ἀποθνῄσκωμεν τῷ κυρίῳ ἀποθνῄσκομεν ἐάν τε οὖν ζῶμεν ἐάν τε ἀποθνῄσκωμεν τοῦ κυρίου ἐσμέν
verses.king_james: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord's.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc enim Christus mortuus est, et resurrexit: ut et mortuorum et vivorum dominetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ χριστὸς καὶ ἀπέθανεν καὶ ἀνέστη καὶ ἀνέζησεν, ἵνα καὶ νεκρῶν καὶ ζώντων κυριεύσῃ
verses.king_james: For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be Lord both of the dead and living.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu autem quid judicas fratrem tuum? aut tu quare spernis fratrem tuum? omnes enim stabimus ante tribunal Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ δὲ τί κρίνεις τὸν ἀδελφόν σου ἢ καὶ σὺ τί ἐξουθενεῖς τὸν ἀδελφόν σου πάντες γὰρ παραστησόμεθα τῷ βήματι τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scriptum est enim: Vivo ego, dicit Dominus, quoniam mihi flectetur omne genu: et omnis lingua confitebitur Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γέγραπται γάρ, ζῶ ἐγώ λέγει κύριος ὅτι ἐμοὶ κάμψει πᾶν γόνυ καὶ πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσεται τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: For it is written, *As* I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque unusquisque nostrum pro se rationem reddet Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα οὖν ἕκαστος ἡμῶν περὶ ἑαυτοῦ λόγον δώσει τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non ergo amplius invicem judicemus: sed hoc judicate magis, ne ponatis offendiculum fratri, vel scandalum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηκέτι οὖν ἀλλήλους κρίνωμεν ἀλλὰ τοῦτο κρίνατε μᾶλλον τὸ μὴ τιθέναι πρόσκομμα τῷ ἀδελφῷ ἢ σκάνδαλον
verses.king_james: Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in *his* brother's way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio, et confido in Domino Jesu, quia nihil commune per ipsum, nisi ei qui existimat quid commune esset, illi commune est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἶδα καὶ πέπεισμαι ἐν κυρίῳ ἰησοῦ ὅτι οὐδὲν κοινὸν δι᾽ ἑαυτοῦ εἰ μὴ τῷ λογιζομένῳ τι κοινὸν εἶναι ἐκείνῳ κοινόν
verses.king_james: I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that *there is* nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him *it is* unclean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim propter cibum frater tuus contristatur, jam non secundum caritatem ambulas. Noli cibo tuo illum perdere, pro quo Christus mortuus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ διὰ βρῶμα ὁ ἀδελφός σου λυπεῖται οὐκέτι κατὰ ἀγάπην περιπατεῖς μὴ τῷ βρώματί σου ἐκεῖνον ἀπόλλυε ὑπὲρ οὗ χριστὸς ἀπέθανεν
verses.king_james: But if thy brother be grieved with *thy* meat, now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non ergo blasphemetur bonum nostrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ βλασφημείσθω οὖν ὑμῶν τὸ ἀγαθόν
verses.king_james: Let not then your good be evil spoken of:

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est enim regnum Dei esca et potus: sed justitia, et pax, et gaudium in Spiritu Sancto:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ βρῶσις καὶ πόσις ἀλλὰ δικαιοσύνη καὶ εἰρήνη καὶ χαρὰ ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui enim in hoc servit Christo, placet Deo, et probatus est hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ ἐν τούτοις δουλεύων τῷ χριστῷ εὐάρεστος τῷ θεῷ καὶ δόκιμος τοῖς ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: For he that in these things serveth Christ *is* acceptable to God, and approved of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque quæ pacis sunt, sectemur: et quæ ædificationis sunt, in invicem custodiamus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα οὖν τὰ τῆς εἰρήνης διώκωμεν καὶ τὰ τῆς οἰκοδομῆς τῆς εἰς ἀλλήλους
verses.king_james: Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli propter escam destruere opus Dei, omnia quidem sunt munda: sed malum est homini, qui per offendiculum manducat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ἕνεκεν βρώματος κατάλυε τὸ ἔργον τοῦ θεοῦ πάντα μὲν καθαρά ἀλλὰ κακὸν τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ τῷ διὰ προσκόμματος ἐσθίοντι
verses.king_james: For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed *are* pure; but *it is* evil for that man who eateth with offence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum est non manducare carnem, et non bibere vinum, neque in quo frater tuus offenditur, aut scandalizatur, aut infirmatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καλὸν τὸ μὴ φαγεῖν κρέα μηδὲ πιεῖν οἶνον μηδὲ ἐν ᾧ ὁ ἀδελφός σου προσκόπτει ἢ σκανδαλίζεται ἤ ἀσθενεῖ
verses.king_james: *It is* good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor *any thing* whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu fidem habes? penes temetipsum habe coram Deo. Beatus qui non judicat semetipsum in eo quod probat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ πίστιν ἔχεις κατὰ σαυτὸν ἔχε ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ μακάριος ὁ μὴ κρίνων ἑαυτὸν ἐν ᾧ δοκιμάζει
verses.king_james: Hast thou faith? have *it* to thyself before God. Happy *is* he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem discernit, si manducaverit, damnatus est: quia non ex fide. Omne autem, quod non est ex fide, peccatum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ διακρινόμενος ἐὰν φάγῃ κατακέκριται ὅτι οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως πᾶν δὲ ὃ οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως ἁμαρτία ἐστίν
verses.king_james: And he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because *he eateth* not of faith: for whatsoever *is* not of faith is sin.
